seo.title: Investment Strategies | diBoaS
seo.description: 10 strategies. Different goals. Different timelines. Different risk levels. Find the strategy that fits your life.

hero.headline: The access they kept. Now yours to choose.
hero.subheadline: Different goals. Different risk levels. Different needs

matrix.instructions: Your goal on the left. Risk appetite on the right.
matrix.note: Not sure? Start with Safe Harbor. Learn first. Switch anytime. No penalties.

safeHarbor.name: Safe Harbor
safeHarbor.tagline: Your safety net that actually grows
safeHarbor.description: This is where you keep money you might need tomorrow. It needs to be there when you need it. No surprises.
safeHarbor.description2: Safe Harbor uses only stable digital dollars. No exposure to digital asset prices. Your $1,000 is designed to stay close to $1,000 while earning returns, though the stablecoins used can fluctuate in value.
safeHarbor.allocation: 50% Sky SSR + 30% Aave V3 + 20% Compound V3
safeHarbor.allocationNote: Your money is distributed across three independent lending systems to reduce risk.
safeHarbor.commonUseCase: First emergency fund. Learning how this works without price swings.

stats.bumpiness: Very smooth. Your balance stayed stable throughout our 4-year test.

safeHarbor.bestFor: People building their first emergency fund. Anyone who wants to learn how this works without price swings.
safeHarbor.riskLevel: Minimal. Risks include possible technical vulnerabilities in the underlying systems and the possibility that the stablecoins used lose their dollar peg. These systems have operated safely for years.

stableGrowth.name: Stable Growth
stableGrowth.description: Your money is split: 70% earns stable returns, 30% participates in digital asset growth. You're accepting some price movement for higher potential returns.
stableGrowth.description2: This is not an emergency fund. It's for money you want to grow faster than inflation, with the understanding that the growth portion will move with market prices.
stableGrowth.allocationNote: The majority stays in stable digital dollars. The Sanctum portion moves with Solana staking returns.
stableGrowth.commonUseCase: Second-layer savings. Already has an emergency fund, wants growth above inflation.
stableGrowth.note: Not designed as a primary emergency fund.

stats.bumpiness: Some waves. At worst, your balance temporarily dropped 8% before recovering.

stableGrowth.bestFor: People comfortable with some price movement who want their safety net to capture growth potential.
stableGrowth.riskLevel: Low. 30% of your balance will move with digital asset prices.

goalKeeper.name: Goal Keeper
goalKeeper.description: Saving for something in the next 2 years? A trip, a wedding, a car? This keeps every dollar working toward your deadline without risking it.
goalKeeper.description2: Saving for something in the next 2 years? A trip, a wedding, a car? This keeps every dollar working toward your deadline without risking it.
goalKeeper.allocation: 60% Sky SSR + 25% Aave V3 + 15% Compound V3
goalKeeper.allocationNote: Distributed across proven lending systems optimized for stability.
goalKeeper.commonUseCase: Specific goal within 2 years: trip, wedding, car.
goalKeeper.bestFor: Anyone with a specific goal and timeline under 2 years.
goalKeeper.riskLevel: Minimal. Designed for capital preservation with steady returns. Risks include possible technical vulnerabilities and the possibility that the stablecoins used lose their dollar peg.

steadyProgress.name: Steady Progress
steadyProgress.description: You have a goal in the next 2 years, but you're okay with some price movement if it means higher returns. The majority stays stable. A portion participates in digital asset growth.
steadyProgress.allocationNote: Most stays stable. The Sanctum portion moves with Solana staking returns.
steadyProgress.commonUseCase: Near-term goal where some price movement is acceptable.

stats.bumpiness: Moderate waves. At worst, your balance temporarily dropped 11% before recovering.

steadyProgress.bestFor: People saving for a goal who can handle seeing their balance dip temporarily.
steadyProgress.riskLevel: Low-Medium. Your balance will move with digital asset prices.

patientBuilder.name: Patient Builder
patientBuilder.tagline: Steady growth for the patient saver
patientBuilder.description: You're thinking 2-5 years out. Maybe a down payment, maybe starting a business. You don't need aggressive growth. You need your money to be there, a bit bigger, when you're ready for it.
patientBuilder.allocation: 50% Sky SSR + 30% Aave V3 + 20% Compound V3
patientBuilder.allocationNote: Same stable systems as Safe Harbor, modeled for medium-term holding. Returns are projected more conservatively for the 2-5 year horizon.
patientBuilder.commonUseCase: Down payment. Business fund. Anything 2-5 years out where predictability matters.
patientBuilder.bestFor: Medium-term savers who prioritize predictability over maximum returns.
patientBuilder.riskLevel: Minimal. Same stable profile, optimized for longer holding. Risks include possible technical vulnerabilities and the possibility that the stablecoins used lose their dollar peg.

balancedBuilder.name: Balanced Builder
balancedBuilder.tagline: Stability and growth in one strategy
balancedBuilder.description: Most of your money stays safe. Some captures digital asset growth. This strategy is designed for people with 3-5 year horizons who understand that prices go up and down.
balancedBuilder.allocation: 60% Sky SSR + 25% Sanctum INF + 15% Jupiter JLP
balancedBuilder.allocationNote: A balanced mix: lending returns plus staking and trading fee income from two Solana systems.
balancedBuilder.commonUseCase: 3-5 year horizon with tolerance for temporary price movement.

stats.bumpiness: Moderate waves. At worst, your balance temporarily dropped 13% before recovering.

balancedBuilder.bestFor: People who understand that prices go up AND down, and can hold through temporary dips.
balancedBuilder.riskLevel: Medium. 40% growth exposure means meaningful swings in both directions.

steadyCompounder.name: Steady Compounder
steadyCompounder.tagline: Let time do the heavy lifting
steadyCompounder.description: You're playing the long game. 5-10 years. You don't need to take big risks because you have time on your side. Steady returns, compounding year after year.
steadyCompounder.allocation: 55% Sky SSR + 30% Aave V3 + 15% Compound V3
steadyCompounder.allocationNote: Optimized for long-term compounding with minimal volatility.
steadyCompounder.commonUseCase: 5-10 year horizon. Prioritizes consistency over maximum upside.
steadyCompounder.bestFor: Long-term savers who want peace of mind over maximum returns.
steadyCompounder.riskLevel: Minimal. Slow and steady. Risks include possible technical vulnerabilities and the possibility that the stablecoins used lose their dollar peg.

wealthAccelerator.name: Wealth Accelerator
wealthAccelerator.tagline: For people who've done the research
wealthAccelerator.description: This is not for everyone. 70% growth exposure means your balance will move significantly with digital asset prices.
wealthAccelerator.description2: You need to be able to watch your balance drop 40%+ and not panic. If that sentence made you uncomfortable, this isn't the right strategy for you.
wealthAccelerator.allocation: 30% Sky SSR + 35% Sanctum INF + 35% Jupiter JLP
wealthAccelerator.allocationNote: Heavily tilted toward growth. Staking returns plus trading fee income from two Solana systems.
wealthAccelerator.commonUseCase: Long-term allocation with high volatility tolerance.

stats.typicalReturn: Highly variable. Could be negative or exceed 50%.
stats.bumpiness: Big swings. At worst, your balance dropped 47% before recovering.

wealthAccelerator.bestFor: Long-term people who can stomach extreme volatility for potentially higher returns.
wealthAccelerator.riskLevel: High. Significant chance of meaningful losses in shorter timeframes.
wealthAccelerator.warning: In thousands of simulations, outcomes ranged from -60% to +200%+. The drop figure (47%) is the worst temporary decline during our test. The range (-60% to +200%+) is the full spread from simulations. Only for people who can tolerate significant losses.

yieldMaximizer.name: Yield Maximizer
yieldMaximizer.description: You want the highest stable returns over 10+ years without exposure to digital asset prices. No growth component. Just optimized returns across all three stable lending systems.
yieldMaximizer.allocation: 45% Sky SSR + 35% Aave V3 + 20% Compound V3
yieldMaximizer.commonUseCase: 10+ year horizon. Maximum stable returns, zero crypto exposure.
yieldMaximizer.bestFor: Conservative long-term wealth builders who want strong returns without price swings.
yieldMaximizer.riskLevel: Minimal. Our highest-returning stable strategy. Risks include possible technical vulnerabilities and the possibility that the stablecoins used lose their dollar peg.

fullThrottle.name: Full Throttle
fullThrottle.description: This is our most aggressive strategy. 85% growth exposure. Designed for a small portion of your portfolio that you're willing to lose entirely.
fullThrottle.description2: The upside? In rare simulated scenarios, returns exceeded 1,000%. The downside? 27% chance of loss. Your balance dropped 66% at its worst moment in our test.
fullThrottle.allocation: 15% Sky SSR + 30% Sanctum INF + 35% Jupiter JLP + 20% Jito
fullThrottle.allocationNote: Maximum growth exposure: staking, trading fees, and MEV rewards across three Solana systems with a minimal stability buffer.
fullThrottle.commonUseCase: Small portfolio allocation with full understanding of potential loss.
fullThrottle.accessRequirements: 6+ months account age. Minimum $1,000 balance. Maximum 20% of your total portfolio. 24-hour waiting period before activation. Risk acknowledgment required.

stats.typicalReturn: Extremely variable. From large losses to extraordinary gains.
stats.bumpiness: Roller coaster. At worst, your balance dropped 66% before recovering.

fullThrottle.bestFor: Experienced savers with high risk tolerance allocating a small portion of their portfolio.
fullThrottle.riskLevel: Very High. Near-total loss possible. Only use what you can afford to lose completely.
fullThrottle.warning: In thousands of simulations, outcomes ranged from -78% to +400%+. The drop figure (66%) is the worst temporary decline during our test. The range (-78% to +400%+) is the full spread from simulations. Never put money here that you need.

allCardsHonestLimitation: All stats are based on historical analysis (May 2022 - December 2025) and thousands of Monte Carlo simulations. For newer protocols, earlier-period returns are estimated using validated proxies based on similar systems. What happened in the past may not happen again. These numbers help you compare strategies, not predict the future.

protocols.intro: Every strategy is built from a combination of these protocols. They're independent, open-source, and you can verify everything yourself.

skySsr.name: Sky SSR
skySsr.summary: Returns generated from lending USDS to borrowers. Variable rate.

aaveV3.name: Aave V3
aaveV3.summary: Returns generated from lending USDC to borrowers. Variable rate. Multiple independent audits.

compoundV3.name: Compound V3
compoundV3.summary: Returns generated from lending USDC to borrowers. Variable rate. One of the oldest DeFi lending protocols.

sanctumInf.name: Sanctum INF
sanctumInf.summary: Returns generated from Solana staking rewards across a basket of liquid staking tokens.

jupiterJlp.name: Jupiter JLP
jupiterJlp.cryptoExposure: Yes, moves with SOL, ETH, BTC prices
jupiterJlp.summary: Returns generated from fees paid by perpetual futures traders. Acts as the counterparty to leveraged trades.

jito.name: Jito
jito.summary: Returns generated from Solana staking rewards plus MEV (Maximal Extractable Value) income.

protocols.belowTable: Protocol names are used for transparency. Their inclusion does not imply endorsement of diBoaS by these protocols. For protocols operating less than 4 years, earlier-period returns are estimated using validated proxy methodologies based on similar systems.
protocols.honestLimitation: Open-source and audited does not mean risk-free. Code can have undiscovered vulnerabilities. Your risk is reduced by spreading your money across multiple independent protocols, but we cannot eliminate it.

fees.belowTable:
Nothing else. No hidden spreads or charges.

Putting money into a strategy costs nothing. We only charge when you take money out. If your money sits in a strategy earning returns, we earn nothing until you exit.
fees.microText: Third-party network fees may apply (typically less than $0.01). For the full fee schedule including transfers and cash-outs, see our fee page.

options.emergency: Emergency fund: Safe Harbor. Money you might need tomorrow stays stable.
options.beatInflation: Outpace inflation: Stable Growth. Your money works harder, but 30% moves with the market.
options.shortTerm: Something in the next 2 years: Goal Keeper (stable) or Steady Progress (with growth). Depends on how you feel about price movement.
options.mediumTerm: Something in 2-5 years: Patient Builder (stable) or Balanced Builder (with growth). More time means you can consider more growth.
options.longTerm: Long-term wealth: Steady Compounder, Wealth Accelerator, Yield Maximizer, or Full Throttle. Your timeline is your biggest advantage.

question2.title: How do you feel about price swings?

options.none: "I don't want any." Stick to the Stable Returns column. Five strategies, zero crypto exposure.
options.understand: "I understand them and can wait out dips." Consider the Growth Potential column. The longer your timeline, the more growth exposure you can consider.
options.unsure: "I'm not sure." Start stable. Learn how everything works with money you're comfortable risking. You can always add growth exposure later.

question3.title: What would you do if your balance dropped 20%?

options.panic: "I'd panic and withdraw." Stable Returns strategies only. That's not a weakness. It's self-awareness.
options.wait: "I'd wait for it to recover." Low-medium growth strategies could work for you.
options.addMore: "I'd add more money." You might be ready for higher growth exposure. That's smart if it's planned. Less smart if it's panic trying to make back what you lost. Make sure you know which one you mean.

howToChoose.brandPromise: We show you both sides, the opportunities and the risks, always.
howToChoose.goldenRule: When in doubt, start safe. You can always move up later. Consider consulting a licensed financial advisor if you're unsure which approach fits your situation.

switch.question: Can I switch strategies?
switch.answer:
Yes, anytime. No penalties. No questions asked.

Here's something to keep in mind: if you switch during a market dip, you might lock in a temporary loss. The best time to switch is when your goals change, not when the market moves.

multiple.question: Can I use multiple strategies at once?
multiple.answer:
Yes. Many people do.

Think of it like different accounts for different purposes: emergency fund in Safe Harbor, vacation savings in Goal Keeper, long-term wealth in Balanced Builder.

rebalancing.question: How does rebalancing work?
rebalancing.answer:
When market movements push your allocation off target (more than 10% drift), we notify you. For example, if your target is 60% stable and 40% growth, and market movements push it to around 55/45 or further, we let you know.

You'll see exactly what changed and why. Then you decide: approve the rebalance, or leave it as is.

We never move your money without your approval.

guaranteed.question: Are these returns guaranteed?
guaranteed.answer:
No. And anyone who guarantees returns is lying to you.

What we can tell you: we tested every strategy across nearly 4 years of real market data (May 2022 - December 2025). The numbers are based on what actually happened and thousands of Monte Carlo simulations.

These numbers help you compare strategies and understand the range of outcomes. They don't predict the future. Start with what you can afford to learn with.

systemProblem.question: What if one of the systems has a problem?
systemProblem.answer:
This is a real risk. These systems are built on code, and code can have vulnerabilities.

We reduce this risk by only using systems that have secured billions of dollars for years, spreading your money across multiple independent systems, and monitoring for unusual activity continuously.

We can't eliminate this risk. No one can. But we can be honest about it.

whereMoneyGoes.question: Where does my money actually go?
whereMoneyGoes.answer:
The protocols behind every strategy are listed on this page with their names, chains, asset types, and track records. No signup required. No hidden information.

Sky SSR, Aave V3, and Compound V3 handle stable returns. Sanctum INF, Jupiter JLP, and Jito handle growth. Every strategy is a specific combination of these protocols with exact percentages shown on each strategy card above.

We chose these protocols because they're transparent, battle-tested, and you can verify everything yourself.

fullThrottleReqs.question: Why are there extra requirements for Full Throttle?
fullThrottleReqs.answer:
Because we want to protect you from yourself.

Full Throttle can lose most of its value. The requirements aren't there to exclude you. They're there to make sure you've thought it through: 6 months of experience, a minimum balance, a cap at 20% of your portfolio, and a 24-hour cooling period.

safe.question: Is my money safe?
safe.answerA:
Your money is secured by you. Your wallet, your keys. No one at diBoaS can access your funds without your authorization.

That said, this isn't a bank account. Your funds work through automated systems built on code. The value can fluctuate, and you could lose some or all of your investment. There is no deposit insurance.

We show you both sides, the opportunities and the risks, always.
safe.answerB:
Your money is protected by multi-party security. Your authorization is required for every transaction. DiBoaS holds a partial key share for recovery purposes, but cannot unilaterally move your funds.

That said, this isn't a bank account. Your funds work through automated systems built on code. The value can fluctuate, and you could lose some or all of your investment. There is no deposit insurance.

We show you both sides, the opportunities and the risks, always.

loseEverything.question: Can I lose everything?
loseEverything.answer:
In stable strategies (Safe Harbor, Goal Keeper, Patient Builder, Steady Compounder, Yield Maximizer): the chance of total loss is extremely low. In nearly 4 years of testing and thousands of simulations, it didn't happen. But "extremely low" is not zero.

In growth strategies: the higher the growth percentage, the wider the range of outcomes. Full Throttle at 85% growth exposure has seen simulated drops exceeding 78%.

The risk is real. We don't minimize it. We help you choose the level that matches what you can handle.

bankDifference.question: How is this different from a bank savings account?
bankDifference.answer:
A bank savings account is insured by the government (up to limits). Your money earns a fixed rate. The bank controls it.

diBoaS strategies use automated lending and staking systems. Returns are variable. Your money is not insured. You control it through your own wallet.

The trade-off: potentially higher returns, but you accept the risk that comes with a different kind of system.

footer.disclaimer: All performance data is based on historical analysis (May 2022 - December 2025) and thousands of Monte Carlo simulations. Past performance does not guarantee future results. Your money is placed in automated systems that carry technical risk, market risk, liquidity risk, and stablecoin depeg risk. Growth strategies involve additional price volatility risk. Growth strategies use protocols on the Solana blockchain; events affecting Solana specifically could impact all growth strategies simultaneously. Only use money you can afford to lose. diBoaS is not a bank and your funds are not insured.
footer.micaArticle68: The value of crypto-assets may fluctuate. You may lose some or all of your money. Crypto-assets are not covered by deposit guarantee schemes.
footer.micaArticle7: This crypto-asset marketing communication has not been reviewed or approved by any competent authority in any Member State of the European Union. The offeror of the crypto-asset is solely responsible for the content of this crypto-asset marketing communication.
footer.aiDisclosure: Certain content on this platform, including market analysis and educational materials, is generated or assisted by artificial intelligence. AI-generated content may contain errors or limitations. Users should verify information independently before making financial decisions.
footer.usDisclosure: diBoaS is a non-custodial interface providing access to decentralized finance protocols. diBoaS is not registered with the SEC, CFTC, FinCEN, or any state regulatory agency. US regulatory treatment of DeFi is evolving. You are responsible for determining whether your use of this interface complies with applicable laws.
footer.fictionalResults: Projected returns, probability estimates, and simulation results on this page are illustrative and do not represent guaranteed outcomes. Calculator projections are based on hypothetical scenarios and historical averages. Actual results will vary.
footer.professionalAdvice: The information on this page is for educational and informational purposes only. It does not constitute investment advice, financial advice, or any other form of professional advice. Consider consulting a licensed financial advisor before making investment decisions.
